$comment: ELF64 SHARED LIBRARY (ET_DYN, .so) for aarch64 Linux -- c171 conformance (the arm64 sibling of elf64-x86_64-linux-dyn, D-LK-ELF-DYN-AARCH64-FLAVOR). CONFIG-ONLY: the `src/link/format/elf.cpp` writer is machine-parameterized -- `elf.type = dyn` routes to the SAME `encodeElfExecDynamic` substrate, whose arm64 arms ALREADY EXIST (the ET_DYN dispatch machine-guard admits machine 183; `relativeRelocTypeFor(183)` = R_AARCH64_RELATIVE = 1027; `globDatTypeFor(183)` = R_AARCH64_GLOB_DAT = 1025; `emitArm64PltStub()` is the 16-byte ADRP+LDR+BR GOT-indirect stub the aarch64 EXEC already uses for its libc `exit` PLT). So this needs NO writer code, only this schema. The dyn divergences (e_type=ET_DYN, e_entry=0, no PT_PHDR/PT_INTERP/.interp, base-0 VAs the loader slides, real-named .dynsym EXPORTS findable via .hash, R_AARCH64_RELATIVE for internal absolute data slots, got-indirect extern data) are identical to the x86_64 .so; only the machine (183 vs 62), the reloc rows (arm64 vs x86_64), and the writer's per-machine RELATIVE/GLOB_DAT/PLT-stub selection differ. A FOREIGN toolchain consumes the output: `aarch64-linux-gnu-gcc main.c -L. -lfoo` links against the DSS-emitted libfoo.so and the program runs under qemu-aarch64 / on the ubuntu-24.04-arm CI leg. Differs from elf64-aarch64-linux-exec in: `elf.type` (dyn vs exec), NO `interpreter` (a .so is loaded by an already-running ld.so; validate() rejects one here), text `virtualAddress` = pageAlign (base-0 image; validate() pins the equality), NO `entryPoint`/`processExit`/`entryCallingConvention`/`processArgs` (a .so has no process entry -- the PIE-executable sibling is exactly ET_DYN plus that entry machinery), `dataImportBinding` got-indirect (copy relocations are exec-only ELF), NO `tlsAccess` and NO tdata/tbss rows (TLS in a .so needs the general-dynamic model -- D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL; every thread_local fails loud at the MIR->LIR gate / linker section gate), `artifactProfiles` ["lib"].
$dataModelComment: Same LP64 identity as the aarch64 exec/rel siblings (64-bit Linux).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: Linux arm64 (AAPCS64) uses the generic LSB-first bit-field allocation -- identical to SysV/GNU gnu_packed for little-endian -- same as the aarch64 ELF siblings.
$externCallDispatchComment: Same call-site shape as the exec image: the walker synthesizes a 16-byte ARM64 PLT stub (ADRP+LDR+BR through the GOT slot, GLOB_DAT eager-bound) per extern FUNCTION, and the call site is a plain direct `BL` to the stub -- pc-relative, so the code needs no fixup under the ET_DYN load slide. D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH / D-LK6-8.
$dataImportBindingComment: c150 (D-LK1-4 + D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT): extern library DATA objects bind GOT-INDIRECT in a shared library -- the walker reserves a GOT slot per imported object and emits R_AARCH64_GLOB_DAT against it; ld.so writes the object's address into the slot and the GotIndirect lowering reads it. Copy relocations are NOT valid inside a .so (the exec owns the one canonical copy); validate() rejects copy-relocation on a dyn schema.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: rodata (read-only, R+X PT_LOAD #1 -- reloc-FREE items only: a reloc-bearing rodata item would need a load-time write into a read-only page, D-LK-DYN-RODATA-ITEM-RELOC fails loud), data (mutable initialized globals, R+W PT_LOAD #2), bss (zero-init, NOBITS tail of PT_LOAD #2), relro (reloc-bearing CONST data, c145 -- merged into `.data` by the dynamic arm so its R_AARCH64_RELATIVE slots land in writable pages). NO tdata/tbss: thread-locals in a shared library need the general-dynamic TLS model (D-LK-DYN-TLS-MODEL, not implemented); the linker's pre-walker gate rejects them loudly by this set's absence.
$artifactProfilesComment: This format SERVES the shared-library profile (plan 06 AP3). The cli profile is NOT served (a .so is not a runnable console binary).

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity for an arm64 shared library. type = ET_DYN (3). e_entry = 0 (no process entry). machine = 183 (EM_AARCH64). NO interpreter (validate() rejects one on dyn). bindNow = true: eager binding, DT_FLAGS_1 = DF_1_NOW + R_AARCH64_GLOB_DAT in .rela.dyn -- the same v1 stance as the exec image.

sections.$comment: Executable code. virtualAddress = 4096 = elf.pageAlign, PINNED by validate(): ET_DYN VAs are BASE-RELATIVE (the loader slides the whole image), and the walker computes baseImageVa = text.virtualAddress - pageAlign, so the equality makes the image base 0 BY CONSTRUCTION -- the gcc .so convention. sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC|SHF_EXECINSTR (6).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Read-only initialized data (string literals, reloc-free const globals). Folded into the R+X PT_LOAD #1 after .text. virtualAddress = 0 -- computed. A reloc-BEARING const global goes to relro instead (c145): RELATIVE fixups must land in writable pages.
sections.name: .rodata

sections.$comment: Writable initialized data. R+W PT_LOAD #2 (W^X preserved). virtualAddress = 0 -- computed.
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: Zero-initialized data. SHT_NOBITS (8); memsz-only tail of PT_LOAD #2. virtualAddress = 0 -- computed.
sections.name: .bss

sections.$comment: Symbol table (debugger support; the LOADER reads .dynsym, which the walker emits unconditionally on this arm). sh_entsize = 24.
sections.name: .symtab

relocations.$comment: 26-bit PC-relative branch-with-link (BL) -- the extern-call reloc on a PLT stub. Legal in ET_DYN `.text` (pc-relative, no DT_TEXTREL). kind = 1 matches arm64.target.json's `call26` row.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_CALL26

relocations.$comment: ADRP page-base pc-relative (high 21 bits) -- data address computation. Legal in `.text` (pc-relative). kind = 2 = arm64.target.json's `adr_prel_pg_hi21`.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_PG_HI21

relocations.$comment: ADD low-12 absolute (no check) -- the pair with ADRP for a full address. kind = 3 = `add_abs_lo12_nc`.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ADD_ABS_LO12_NC

relocations.$comment: abs64 -- legal in DATA items only on this arm: the walker patches the slot with the base-relative value AND emits an R_AARCH64_RELATIVE (1027) row for it (the load slide completes the address). kind = 4 = `abs64`.
relocations.name: R_AARCH64_ABS64
